### Install NRP Command-Line Tool

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library

Installs the 'nrp-cmd' package, which includes the NRP Python client library and a CLI application for interactive use.

```bash
pip install nrp-cmd
```

--------------------------------

### Verify Prerequisites for CESNET Invenio Repository

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation

This snippet demonstrates how to verify the installation of all necessary prerequisites for the CESNET Invenio repository. It includes commands to check versions of uname, Python, Node.js, pnpm, Docker, Docker Compose, Git, uv, and Bash. Ensure all listed tools are installed and meet the version requirements.

```shell
❯ uname -a
Darwin MacBookPro.localdomain 25.0.0 Darwin Kernel Version 25.0.0

❯ python --version
Python 3.13.2

❯ node --version
v20.9.0

❯ pnpm --version
10.19.0

❯ docker run hello-world
Hello from Docker!

❯ docker-compose --version
Docker Compose version v2.39.2

❯ git --version
git version 2.50.1

❯ uv --version
uv 0.8.18

❯ bash --version
5.3.3
```

--------------------------------

### NRP Commandline Tool Examples

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/commandline

Demonstrates common operations using the NRP commandline tool, including adding repositories, creating and publishing records with files and metadata, and performing bulk record operations like scanning, downloading, and updating.

```bash
# add a repository, will ask for token
nrp-cmd add repository https://data.narodni-repozitar.cz/ catchall --default

# create a new record and upload file to it. Store the record url to variable rec
nrp-cmd create record --community brno \
        '{"title": "My Record"}' \
        /tmp/myfile.txt '{"title":"My file"}' --set rec

# or with metadata in file
nrp-cmd create record --community brno \
        metadata.json \
        /tmp/myfile.txt /tmp/myfile-metadata.json --set rec

# publish the record
nrp-cmd publish record @rec

# get all records with publisher being 'myorg'
nrp-cmd scan records "metadata.publisher:myorg" --set ids

# download them together with files to the current directory as a backup
nrp-cmd download record @ids

# After carefully inspecting them, 
# change the publisher to 'myorg2' inside all the records
nrp-cmd update record @ids --path metadata.publisher myorg2
```

--------------------------------

### Install Repository Dependencies

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation/run_instance

Installs all required Python and NPM dependencies for the repository. It also collects and builds frontend assets. This command can take a significant amount of time on the first run.

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Install backend and frontend dependencies
./run.sh install
```

--------------------------------

### Download and Run OARepo Repository Installer

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation/create_instance

Downloads the repository installer script from the OARepo GitHub repository and executes it to scaffold a new project directory. The installer prompts for repository details such as name, description, and language configuration.

```bash
curl -o repository_installer.sh https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oarepo/oarepo/refs/heads/main/tools/repository_installer.sh
bash repository_installer.sh instruments
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize i18next Configuration

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

Setup script for i18next in a React environment, including language detection and resource initialization.

```javascript
import i18n from "i18next";
import LanguageDetector from "i18next-browser-languagedetector";
import { translations } from "./messages";
import { initReactI18next } from "react-i18next";
 
const options = {
  fallbackLng: "en",
  returnEmptyString: false,
  debug: process.env.NODE_ENV === "development",
  resources: translations,
  keySeparator: false,
  nsSeparator: false,
  detection: {
    order: ["htmlTag"],
    caches: [],
  },
  react: {
    transKeepBasicHtmlNodesFor: [],
  },
};
 
const i18next = i18n.createInstance();
i18next.use(LanguageDetector).use(initReactI18next).init(options);
 
export { i18next };
```

--------------------------------

### Jinja Page Composition Example

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/templating

A comprehensive example demonstrating how Jinja's inheritance, includes, and macros can be combined to construct a complete page, integrating reusable components and extending base layouts.

```jinja
{# records/detail.html #}
{% extends "invenio_theme/page.html" %}
 
{% from "macros/records.html" import record_card, metadata_table %}
 
{% block page_content %}
  {% include "components/breadcrumbs.html" %}
  <div class="ui container">
    {{ record_card(record, show_metadata=true) }}
  </div>
{% endblock %}
```

--------------------------------

### Complete Workflow Examples

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_requests

End-to-end examples showing how to programmatically request publication or metadata edits for records, including checking for applicable request types.

```python
async def publish_with_approval(record):
    request_types = await client.requests.applicable_requests(record)
    # Logic to create and submit request
    request = await client.requests.create(publish_type, payload={}, submit=True)
    return request
```

--------------------------------

### List requests on a record

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/commandline

Commands and example output for listing and inspecting requests associated with a specific record.

```bash
nrp-cmd list requests <pid>
```

```yaml
documents_publish_draft:
  type_id: documents_publish_draft
  links:
    actions:
      create: https://127.0.0.1:5000/api/docs/b465x-wz855/draft/requests/documents_publish_draft
  requests:
  - id: 0c224d67-f8a1-4a51-bf15-a75bfd53ceb2
    created: '2024-03-16T16:36:42.538694+00:00'
    updated: '2024-03-16T16:36:42.545675+00:00'
    links:
      actions:
        submit: https://127.0.0.1:5000/api/requests/0c224d67-f8a1-4a51-bf15-a75bfd53ceb2/actions/submit
      self: https://127.0.0.1:5000/api/requests/extended/0c224d67-f8a1-4a51-bf15-a75bfd53ceb2
      comments: https://127.0.0.1:5000/api/requests/extended/0c224d67-f8a1-4a51-bf15-a75bfd53ceb2/comments
      timeline: https://127.0.0.1:5000/api/requests/extended/0c224d67-f8a1-4a51-bf15-a75bfd53ceb2/timeline
    revision_id: 2
    type: documents_publish_draft
    title: ''
    number: '2'
    status: created
    is_closed: false
    is_open: false
    expires_at: null
    is_expired: false
    created_by:
      user: '1'
    receiver:
      group: curator
    topic:
      documents_draft: b465x-wz855
```

--------------------------------

### Install NRP Commandline Tool with pip and virtualenv

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/commandline

Instructions for installing the NRP commandline tool using Python's pip and a virtual environment. It includes steps to create the environment, activate it, and install the package.

```bash
python3 -m venv nrp-cmd
source nrp-cmd/bin/activate
pip install nrp-cmd
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Redux Store and Slices with Redux Toolkit

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/js_assets/react/state_management

Demonstrates the setup of a Redux store and a feature slice using Redux Toolkit. It includes an example of how to dispatch actions and select state within a functional React component.

```javascript
import { configureStore, createSlice } from "@reduxjs/toolkit";
import { Provider, useSelector, useDispatch } from "react-redux";

// Create a slice
const searchSlice = createSlice({
  name: "search",
  initialState: { query: "", results: [] },
  reducers: {
    setQuery: (state, action) => {
      state.query = action.payload;
    },
  },
});

// Configure store
const store = configureStore({
  reducer: {
    search: searchSlice.reducer,
  },
});

// Use in components
export const SearchComponent = () => {
  const query = useSelector((state) => state.search.query);
  const dispatch = useDispatch();

  return (
    <input
      value={query}
      onChange={(e) => dispatch(searchSlice.actions.setQuery(e.target.value))}
    />
  );
};
```

--------------------------------

### Create a Record using Synchronous Python Client

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library

Demonstrates how to use the synchronous NRP Python client to connect to a repository and create a new research data record. Requires the 'nrp-cmd' library to be installed.

```python
from nrp_cmd.sync_client import get_sync_client

# Connect to a repository
client = get_sync_client("https://your-repository.org")

# Create a new record
record = client.records.create({
    "metadata": {
        "title": "My Research Data",
        "creators": [{"name": "John Doe"}],
        "resource_type": {"id": "dataset"}
    }
})

print(f"Created record: {record.id}")
```

--------------------------------

### Complete Request Lifecycle Example

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/sync_library_requests

Illustrates the complete lifecycle of a request, from creating a draft record to submitting a publish request and monitoring its status. This example requires appropriate permissions to execute.

```python
def complete_request_lifecycle():
    """Demonstrate complete request lifecycle."""
    # 1. Create draft record
    draft = client.records.create({
        "metadata": {
            "title": "Request Lifecycle Demo",
            "creators": [{"person_or_org": {"name": "Demo User"}}],
            "resource_type": {"id": "dataset"}
        }
    })
    print(f"✓ Created draft: {draft.id}")
    
    # 2. Check applicable requests
    request_types = client.requests.applicable_requests(draft)
    print(f"✓ Available request types: {len(request_types.hits)}")
    
    # 3. Create publish request
    publish_type = next(
        rt for rt in request_types.hits 
        if rt.type_id == "publish_draft"
    )
    request = client.requests.create(
        publish_type,
        payload={"message": "Ready for publication"}
    )
    print(f"✓ Created request: {request.id} (status: {request.status})")
    
    # 4. Submit request
    submitted = client.requests.submit(request)
    print(f"✓ Submitted request (status: {submitted.status})")
    
    # 5. List requests for this record
    record_requests = client.requests.all(topic=draft)
    print(f"✓ Record has {len(record_requests.hits.hits)} request(s)")
    
    # 6. Curator accepts (if you have permission)
    # accepted = client.requests.accept(submitted)
    # print(f"✓ Accepted request (status: {accepted.status})")
    
    return request
 
# Run the demo
request = complete_request_lifecycle()
```

--------------------------------

### Run the Repository

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation/run_instance

Starts the repository, including background services like the database and search engine, as well as background tasks. The repository is operational once pending tasks are processed.

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Start the repository and its services
./run.sh run
```

--------------------------------

### Set Up Services and Database

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation/run_instance

Performs the initial setup for the database and other required services. The '-N' option skips adding demo data. If containers are destroyed, '.invenio.private' may need adjustment.

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Set up services and database, skipping demo data
./run.sh services setup -N
```

--------------------------------

### Create Bash Alias for NRP Commandline Tool

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/commandline

Provides examples of how to create a bash alias for the `nrp-cmd` tool in `.bashrc` or `.zshrc` files for easier access. It shows aliases for both a direct path and using `uvx`.

```bash
alias nrp-cmd="<path-to-nrp-cmd>/bin/nrp-cmd"
# or
alias nrp-cmd="uvx nrp-cmd"
```

--------------------------------

### Import i18next Instance in React

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

Example of importing the configured i18next instance into a React component using the defined webpack alias.

```javascript
import i18next from "@translations/my_module/i18next";
```

--------------------------------

### Create Records with Various Configurations

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

Demonstrates different methods for creating records, including basic metadata, community-scoped creation, workflow integration, model specification, and cloning from existing records.

```python
# Basic Record Creation
record = await client.records.create({
    "metadata": {
        "title": "My Research Data",
        "creators": [{"person_or_org": {"type": "personal", "family_name": "Doe", "given_name": "John"}}],
        "resource_type": {"id": "dataset"},
        "publication_date": "2024-01-15"
    }
})

# Create in a Community
record = await client.records.create(
    {"metadata": {"title": "Community Research", "creators": [{"person_or_org": {"name": "Jane Smith"}}], "resource_type": {"id": "publication"}}},
    community="my-community-slug"
)

# Create with Workflow
record = await client.records.create(
    {"metadata": {"title": "Workflow Test", "creators": [{"person_or_org": {"name": "Test User"}}], "resource_type": {"id": "dataset"}}},
    community="my-community",
    workflow="review"
)

# Create with Specific Model
datasets_client = client.records.with_model("datasets")
record = await datasets_client.create({
    "metadata": {"title": "Dataset Record", "creators": [{"person_or_org": {"name": "Researcher"}}]}
})

# Create Without Files
record = await client.records.create(
    {"metadata": {"title": "Metadata Only", "creators": [{"person_or_org": {"name": "Author"}}], "resource_type": {"id": "other"}}},
    files_enabled=False
)

# Create from Existing Data
existing = await client.records.read("abc-123")
new_record = await client.records.create({
    "metadata": existing.metadata
})
```

--------------------------------

### Define Unextractable Strings in JinjaX

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

Example of a jinjax_messages.jinja file used to collect strings that automated Babel extractors cannot detect.

```jinja
{{ _("Dataset") }}
{{ _("Add new item") }}
{{ _("Advanced search") }}
```

--------------------------------

### Compile Translation Files

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

Command to compile .po translation files into .mo files for runtime usage.

```bash
./run.sh cli translations compile
```

--------------------------------

### Clone and Navigate Repository

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation/run_instance

Clones a Git repository to the local machine and changes the directory to the repository's root. This is the initial step before running any setup or build commands.

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Clone the repository
git clone <repository-url>

# Navigate into the repository directory
cd <repository-name>
```

--------------------------------

### Watch Frontend Assets

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/model_ui/tutorial

Starts a watcher process to automatically rebuild frontend assets upon file changes. This is essential for seeing JavaScript modifications in real-time.

```bash
./run.sh cli assets watch

```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Async Records Client

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

Establishes a connection to the NRP repository using the asynchronous client library to access the records interface.

```python
from nrp_cmd.async_client import get_async_client

client = await get_async_client("https://your-repository.org")
records_client = client.records
```

--------------------------------

### Check Repository Requirements

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation/run_instance

Verifies that all necessary system prerequisites for the repository are installed and correctly configured. It checks versions of Python, Pipenv, Docker, Node, NPM, ImageMagick, and Git.

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Check all prerequisites for the repository
./run.sh cli check-requirements -d
```

--------------------------------

### Get Async Client

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library

Gets an asynchronous client for a repository.

```APIDOC
## Get Async Client

### Description
Gets an asynchronous client for a repository.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`/`

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **repository** (string) - Required - The name of the repository.
- **refresh** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to refresh the client.
- **config** (object) - Optional - Configuration for the client.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **client** (AsyncClient) - The asynchronous client instance.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "client": "<async_client_instance>"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Translation Catalogs via NPM Scripts

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

Configuration for package.json scripts to automate the extraction, compilation, and initialization of translation catalogs.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "extract_messages": "i18next-scanner --config i18next-scanner.config.js '../../js/**/**/*.{js,jsx}' '!../../js/**/node_modules/**'",
    "postextract_messages": "node ./scripts/postExtractMessages.js",
    "compile_catalog": "node ./scripts/compileCatalog.js",
    "init_catalog": "node ./scripts/initCatalog"
  }
}
```

```bash
cd ui/mymodule/translations/my_module
npm run extract_messages
npm run init_catalog
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Localizable Strings in Python

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

How to import and use gettext utilities to mark strings for translation within Python code.

```python
from flask_babel import gettext as _
# or
from flask_babel import lazy_gettext as _

title = _("Datasets")
description = _("Create, publish, and manage datasets.")
```

--------------------------------

### Create and Publish Draft Record

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

This snippet demonstrates how to create a draft record with specified metadata and then publish it. It uses the `client.records.create` method to initialize the draft and `client.records.publish` to make it live. The output is the URL of the published record.

```python
draft = await client.records.create({
    "metadata": {
        "title": "Ready to Publish",
        "creators": [{"person_or_org": {"name": "Author"}}],
        "resource_type": {"id": "dataset"}
    }
})

# Publish the draft
published = await client.records.publish(draft)
print(f"Published URL: {published.links.self_html}")
```

--------------------------------

### GET /records/{id}

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

Retrieves a record by its ID or URL. Supports reading draft or published versions and applying model-specific filters.

```APIDOC
## GET /records/{id}

### Description
Reads a specific record from the repository. Can be used to fetch draft or published versions.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/records/{id}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **id** (string) - Required - The unique identifier or full URL of the record.

#### Query Parameters
- **expand** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to expand related resources.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **metadata** (object) - The record metadata.
- **created** (string) - Creation timestamp.
- **revision_id** (string) - Current revision identifier.

#### Response Example
{
  "metadata": { "title": "Example Record" },
  "created": "2024-01-15T00:00:00Z",
  "revision_id": "1"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Handle repository errors in Python

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

Demonstrates how to catch and differentiate between validation errors and network-related communication errors when interacting with the repository client.

```python
from nrp_cmd.errors import (
    RepositoryCommunicationError,
    RepositoryClientError
)
 
try:
    record = await client.records.create(invalid_data)
except RepositoryClientError as e:
    print(f"Validation error: {e}")
    # Handle validation errors
except RepositoryCommunicationError as e:
    print(f"Network error: {e}")
    # Handle network issues
```

--------------------------------

### Install npm Dependencies

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/webpack

Command to run after modifying npm dependencies in the webpack.py configuration to install the specified packages.

```bash
invenio webpack install
```

--------------------------------

### Scan Records to JSONL Output

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/commandline

Scans records and outputs them in JSONL format (JSON objects on separate lines), suitable for line-by-line processing. This example pipes the output to a while loop for individual record processing.

```bash
nrp-cmd scan records --output-format jsonl | while read REC ; do 
  echo $REC   # process the record
done
```

--------------------------------

### Complete Request Lifecycle Example

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_requests

Demonstrates the full lifecycle of a request, from creating a draft record to submitting and managing a publish request.

```APIDOC
## Request Lifecycle Example

```python
async def complete_request_lifecycle():
    """Demonstrate complete request lifecycle."""
    # 1. Create draft record
    draft = await client.records.create({
        "metadata": {
            "title": "Request Lifecycle Demo",
            "creators": [{"person_or_org": {"name": "Demo User"}}],
            "resource_type": {"id": "dataset"}
        }
    })
    print(f"✓ Created draft: {draft.id}")
    
    # 2. Check applicable requests
    request_types = await client.requests.applicable_requests(draft)
    print(f"✓ Available request types: {len(request_types.hits)}")
    
    # 3. Create publish request
    publish_type = next(
        rt for rt in request_types.hits 
        if rt.type_id == "publish_draft"
    )
    request = await client.requests.create(
        publish_type,
        payload={"message": "Ready for publication"}
    )
    print(f"✓ Created request: {request.id} (status: {request.status})")
    
    # 4. Submit request
    submitted = await client.requests.submit(request)
    print(f"✓ Submitted request (status: {submitted.status})")
    
    # 5. List requests for this record
    record_requests = await client.requests.all(topic=draft)
    print(f"✓ Record has {len(record_requests.hits.hits)} request(s)")
    
    # 6. Curator accepts (if you have permission)
    # accepted = await client.requests.accept(submitted)
    # print(f"✓ Accepted request (status: {accepted.status})")
    
    return request
 
# Run the demo
request = await complete_request_lifecycle()
```
```

--------------------------------

### Run Script Usage and Commands

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation/run_instance

Displays the usage and available commands for the 'run.sh' script. This script is central to managing the repository's lifecycle, including installation, service management, and CLI operations.

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Execute the run script to display help and commands
./run.sh --help
```

--------------------------------

### Example Webpack Aliases JSON (JSON)

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/js_assets/react/components

An example of the 'aliases' section within a webpack configuration JSON file. It shows how different packages are mapped to '@js/' import paths, enabling access to their React components.

```json
{
  "aliases": {
    "@js/invenio_app_rdm": "js/invenio_app_rdm",
    "@js/oarepo_ui": "js/oarepo_ui",
    "@js/oarepo_vocabularies_ui": "js/oarepo_vocabularies_ui",
    "@js/invenio_communities": "js/invenio_communities",
    "@js/invenio_requests": "js/invenio_requests",
    "@js/invenio_rdm_records": "js/invenio_rdm_records",
    ...
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure InvenioRDM Repository using invenio.cfg

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/configure

Demonstrates how to initialize an NRP repository configuration using the oarepo.config helper module. This script sets up internationalization, UI parameters, cron jobs, and custom model registration within the standard Flask configuration file.

```python
# invenio.cfg
from invenio_i18n import lazy_gettext as _
from oarepo import config
 
# localization / internationalization
config.initialize_i18n()
 
config.configure_generic_parameters()
 
config.configure_ui(
    code="myrepo",
    name=_("My NRP Repository"),
    description=_("My NRP-based repository for research data."),
)
 
config.configure_cron()
config.configure_stats()
 
import os
os.environ["DYLD_FALLBACK_LIBRARY_PATH"]="/opt/homebrew/lib"
 
# datasets model registration
from mymodel import mymodel_model
 
mymodel_model.register()
DASHBOARD_RECORD_CREATE_URL = "/mymodel/uploads/new"
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Localizable Strings in Jinja Templates

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

Techniques for marking strings in Jinja templates using inline function calls or translation blocks.

```jinja2
<!-- Inline call -->
<h1>{{ _("Search results") }}</h1>

<!-- Trans block for complex text -->
{% trans %}
This dataset is part of the national metadata directory.
{% endtrans %}
```

--------------------------------

### Render React Welcome Banner Component

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/webpack

An example of a JavaScript entry point that uses React to render a welcome banner component into a specific DOM element.

```javascript
// ui/components/semantic-ui/js/WelcomeBanner/index.js
import ReactDOM from "react-dom";
import { WelcomeBanner } from "./WelcomeBanner";

function renderWelcomeBanner() {
  const element = document.getElementById("welcome-banner");
  if (element) {
    ReactDOM.render(<WelcomeBanner />, element);
  }
}

renderWelcomeBanner();
```

--------------------------------

### POST /records

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

Creates a new record in the repository. Supports optional community assignment, workflow selection, and model-specific configurations.

```APIDOC
## POST /records

### Description
Creates a new record in the NRP repository. Allows for metadata definition and optional configuration for communities, workflows, and file handling.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/records

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **metadata** (object) - Required - The record metadata including title, creators, and resource type.
- **community** (string) - Optional - The slug of the community to associate the record with.
- **workflow** (string) - Optional - The workflow process to trigger (e.g., 'review').
- **files_enabled** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to enable file uploads for this record.

### Request Example
{
  "metadata": {
    "title": "My Research Data",
    "creators": [{"person_or_org": {"family_name": "Doe", "given_name": "John"}}],
    "resource_type": {"id": "dataset"}
  }
}

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **id** (string) - The unique identifier of the created record.
- **links** (object) - Navigation links for the record.

#### Response Example
{
  "id": "abc-123",
  "links": { "self_": "https://repository.org/api/records/abc-123" }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Publishing Records API

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

Documentation for publishing draft records to make them publicly accessible.

```APIDOC
## POST /api/records/published_records

### Description
Publishes a draft record, making it available to the public.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/api/records/published_records`

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **record** (object) - Required - The draft record object to publish.
  - **id** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the draft record.
  - **etag** (string) - Required - The ETag (version) of the draft record.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **message** (string) - Confirmation message indicating the record was published.
- **published_record** (object) - The newly published record.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Draft record published successfully.",
  "published_record": {
    "id": "published-abc-123",
    "metadata": {
      "title": "Published Title"
    }
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Record Status and Filtering

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/sync_library

Examples of filtering operations between draft and published records.

```python
# Work only with draft records
draft_client = client.records.draft_records
drafts = draft_client.search(q="title:test")

# Work only with published records  
published_client = client.records.published_records
published = published_client.search(q="title:test")
```

--------------------------------

### Watch Frontend Assets

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation/run_instance

Starts the frontend assets builder in watch mode. This command monitors frontend code (LESS, JS, React) for changes and automatically rebuilds affected assets, useful during development.

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Start the frontend assets builder in watch mode
./run.sh cli assets watch
```

--------------------------------

### Update Records with Version Control

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

Covers updating record metadata, including optional version verification to prevent overwriting concurrent changes.

```python
# Basic Update
record = await client.records.draft_records.read("abc-123")
record.metadata["title"] = "Updated Title"
updated = await client.records.draft_records.update(record)

# Update with Version Check
try:
    updated = await client.records.draft_records.update(record, verify_version=True)
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Update failed - record was modified: {e}")

# Update Without Version Check
updated = await client.records.draft_records.update(record, verify_version=False)
```

--------------------------------

### Extract Translation Strings

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

Command to trigger the extraction process for all translatable strings, including those in jinjax_messages.jinja.

```bash
./run.sh cli translations extract
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Supported Languages

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

Configuration snippet for defining supported languages in the Invenio application settings.

```python
I18N_LANGUAGES = [
    ("en", "English"),
    ("cs", "Čeština"),
    ("de", "Deutsch"),
]
```

--------------------------------

### Create Initial Administrator User

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation/run_instance

Creates an initial administrator user with the specified email address. The '-a' flag makes the user an administrator, and '-c' confirms the creation.

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Create an initial administrator user
./run.sh invenio users create -a -c admin@test.com
```

--------------------------------

### Get Repository Variable

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/commandline

Retrieves and prints a variable to standard output. Supports output formatting and an option to retrieve only a single value.

```bash
nrp-cmd get variable <name> [--output-format table|json|yaml] [--single-value]
```

--------------------------------

### Complete Workflow: Create, Upload, Publish Dataset

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

This function encapsulates a common workflow: creating a dataset draft, uploading a data file to it, and then publishing the dataset. It takes a title and a data file as input and returns the published record.

```python
# Complete workflow
async def create_and_publish_dataset(title, data_file):
    # 1. Create draft
    draft = await client.records.create({
        "metadata": {
            "title": title,
            "creators": [{"person_or_org": {"name": "Researcher"}}],
            "resource_type": {"id": "dataset"},
            "publication_date": "2024-01-15"
        }
    })
    
    # 2. Upload data file
    file = await client.files.upload(
        draft,
        key=data_file.name,
        metadata={"description": "Dataset file"},
        source=data_file,
        progress=f"Uploading {data_file.name}"
    )
    
    # 3. Publish
    published = await client.records.publish(draft)
    
    return published
 
# Use it
published = await create_and_publish_dataset(
    "Climate Data 2024",
    "climate_data.csv"
)
print(f"Published at: {published.links.self_html}")
```

--------------------------------

### List All Variables

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/commandline

Lists all stored variables. The output can be directed to a file and formatted as table, JSON, or YAML.

```bash
nrp-cmd list variables [--output <fname>] [--output-format table|json|yaml]
```

--------------------------------

### Example JSON for Object Data Type

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/model_backend/model_reference

Provides a valid JSON example demonstrating the structure for the 'object' data type, including nested 'author' and 'funding_info' objects.

```json
{
  "author": {
    "name": "Dr. Jane Smith",
    "orcid": "0000-0002-1825-0097",
    "affiliation": "Massachusetts Institute of Technology"
  },
  "funding_info": {
    "agency": "National Science Foundation",
    "grant_number": "NSF-2023-1234",
    "amount": 250000.00
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### MacOS Specific Configuration for Invenio

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation/run_instance

A configuration snippet for MacOS users to patch the 'invenio.cfg' file. It sets the DYLD_FALLBACK_LIBRARY_PATH environment variable to help Invenio find necessary libraries when installed via Homebrew.

```python
# hack to get invenio working on MacOS 26
import os
os.environ["DYLD_FALLBACK_LIBRARY_PATH"]="/opt/homebrew/lib"
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Repository Services and Prerequisites

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/installation/create_instance

Utilizes the project's run.sh script to manage Docker services and verify that all necessary system dependencies, such as Python, Pipenv, Docker, and Node.js, are correctly installed and configured.

```bash
cd instruments
./run.sh services start
./run.sh cli check-requirements -d
```

--------------------------------

### GET /files/download

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_files

Downloads a file from a record to a specified local sink.

```APIDOC
## GET /files/download

### Description
Downloads a file from a record or file URL to a local destination.

### Method
GET

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **file_or_url** (string) - Required - The file object or URL.
- **sink** (string) - Required - The local destination path.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - Download completion status.
```

--------------------------------

### Create a Record using Asynchronous Python Client

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library

Shows how to use the asynchronous NRP Python client to connect to a repository and create a new research data record. This approach is suitable for high-performance applications and concurrent operations. Requires the 'nrp-cmd' library.

```python
import asyncio
from nrp_cmd.async_client import get_async_client

async def main():
    # Connect to a repository
    client = await get_async_client("https://your-repository.org")
    
    # Create a new record
    record = await client.records.create({
        "metadata": {
            "title": "My Research Data",
            "creators": [{"name": "John Doe"}],
            "resource_type": {"id": "dataset"}
        }
    })
    
    print(f"Created record: {record.id}")

asyncio.run(main())
```

--------------------------------

### Perform Basic Record and File Operations

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/sync_library

Demonstrates initializing a client, creating a record, uploading a file, and publishing the record.

```python
from nrp_cmd.sync_client import get_sync_client

# Get a client for a repository
client = get_sync_client("https://your-repository.org")

# Create a new record
record = client.records.create({
    "metadata": {
        "title": "My First Record",
        "creators": [{"name": "John Doe"}],
        "resourceType": {"id": "dataset"}
    }
})

print(f"Created record: {record.id}")

# Upload a file
file = client.files.upload(
    record,
    key="data.csv",
    metadata={"description": "My data file"},
    source="path/to/data.csv"
)

print(f"Uploaded file: {file.key}")

# Publish the record
published = client.records.publish(record)
print(f"Published record: {published.id}")
```

--------------------------------

### Conditional File Deletion

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/sync_library_files

Deletes files from a record based on a specified condition. In this example, it deletes files whose keys start with 'temp_'. This allows for selective removal of files.

```python
# Delete files matching condition
files = client.files.list(record)

for file in files:
    # Delete only temporary files
    if file.key.startswith("temp_"):
        client.files.delete(file)
        print(f"✓ Deleted temporary file: {file.key}")
```

--------------------------------

### Perform Basic Record and File Operations

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library

Demonstrates the core workflow of initializing an async client, creating a record, uploading a file, and publishing the record.

```python
import asyncio
from nrp_cmd.async_client import get_async_client
 
async def main():
    # Get a client for a repository
    client = await get_async_client("https://your-repository.org")
    
    # Create a new record
    record = await client.records.create({
        "metadata": {
            "title": "My First Record",
            "creators": [{"name": "John Doe"}],
            "resourceType": {"id": "dataset"}
        }
    })
    
    print(f"Created record: {record.id}")
    
    # Upload a file
    file = await client.files.upload(
        record,
        key="data.csv",
        metadata={"description": "My data file"},
        source="path/to/data.csv"
    )
    
    print(f"Uploaded file: {file.key}")
    
    # Publish the record
    published = await client.records.publish(record)
    print(f"Published record: {published.id}")
 
# Run the async function
asyncio.run(main())
```

--------------------------------

### Search Records

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

Covers various search patterns including basic keyword search, pagination, sorting, faceted filtering, and model-specific scoping.

```python
# Basic Search
results = await client.records.search(q="climate change")

# Paginated Search
page1 = await client.records.search(q="dataset", page=1, size=10)
page2 = await client.records.next_page(record_list=page1)

# Sorting
results = await client.records.search(q="machine learning", sort="newest")

# Facets/Filters
results = await client.records.search(q="climate", facets={"resource_type": "dataset", "access_right": "open"})

# Model specific
datasets = client.records.with_model("datasets")
results = await datasets.draft_records.search(q="temperature")
```

--------------------------------

### List Requests on a Record

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/commandline

Lists all requests associated with a specific record.

```APIDOC
## List Requests on a Record

### Description
Lists all requests associated with a specific record.

### Method
CLI Command

### Endpoint
nrp-cmd list requests <pid>

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **pid** (string) - Required - The record identifier.

### Request Example
```bash
nrp-cmd list requests <pid>
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
A list of requests associated with the record.

#### Response Example
```json
[
  {
    "id": "0c224d67-f8a1-4a51-bf15-a75bfd53ceb2",
    "created": "2024-03-16T16:36:42.538694+00:00",
    "updated": "2024-03-16T16:36:42.545675+00:00",
    "type": "documents_publish_draft",
    "title": "",
    "number": "2",
    "status": "created",
    "is_closed": false,
    "is_open": false,
    "expires_at": null,
    "is_expired": false,
    "created_by": {
      "user": "1"
    },
    "receiver": {
      "group": "curator"
    },
    "topic": {
      "documents_draft": "b465x-wz855"
    }
  }
]
```
```

--------------------------------

### Handle Multiple Repositories

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/sync_library

Demonstrates connecting to multiple repositories and transferring records between them.

```python
# Connect to multiple repositories
repo1 = get_sync_client("https://repo1.org")
repo2 = get_sync_client("https://repo2.org")

# Search draft records in both
results1 = repo1.records.draft_records.search(q="climate")
results2 = repo2.records.draft_records.search(q="climate")

# Copy record from one to another
record1 = results1.hits.hits[0]
record2 = repo2.records.create(record1.metadata)
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Translation Catalogs via CLI

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

Commands to extract, initialize, update, and compile translation catalogs. These operations are essential for maintaining .po files and generating .mo files for the application.

```bash
# Extract strings
./run.sh cli translations extract

# Initialize new language (e.g., Czech)
./run.sh cli translations init -l cs

# Update existing .po files
./run.sh cli translations update

# Compile translations to .mo files
./run.sh cli translations compile
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Files Client

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_files

Demonstrates how to initialize the asynchronous client and access the files interface.

```python
from nrp_cmd.async_client import get_async_client

client = await get_async_client("https://your-repository.org")
files_client = client.files
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Translations in React Components

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

Demonstrates how to use the i18next t() function for simple strings and the Trans component for complex translations containing HTML elements.

```javascript
import i18next from "@translations/my_module/i18next";

export function MyComponent() {
  return (
    <div>
      <h1>{i18next.t("welcome_message")}</h1>
      <p>{i18next.t("description")}</p>
    </div>
  );
}

import { Trans } from "react-i18next";

export function TransComponent() {
  return (
    <Trans i18n={i18next}>
      Learn more about <a href="/docs">our documentation</a>.
    </Trans>
  );
}
```

--------------------------------

### Applicable Request Serialization Format

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/workflows/workflows

Example JSON structure representing the serialized list of applicable request types for a record.

```json
[
 {
  "type_id": "delete_published_record",
  "links": {
   "actions": {
    "create": "https://docs.test.du.cesnet.cz/api/docs/f7cak-7b568/requests/delete_published_record"
   }
  },
  "description": "Request deletion of published record",
  "name": "Delete record",
  "dangerous": true,
  "editable": false,
  "has_form": true,
  "stateful_name": "Delete record",
  "stateful_description": "Click to permanently delete the record."
 }
]
```

--------------------------------

### Add Local Repository for Development

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/commandline

Specific command to add a locally developed repository using `nrp-cmd`, which bypasses TLS certificate verification, useful for testing.

```bash
nrp-cmd add repository https://127.0.0.1:5000/ --no-verify-tls
```

--------------------------------

### Read Records by ID, URL, and Model

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

Shows how to retrieve draft or published records using identifiers, full URLs, or specific repository models, including optional query parameters.

```python
# Read by ID
record = await client.records.draft_records.read("abc-def-ghi")

# Read by URL
from yarl import URL
record_url = URL("https://repository.org/api/records/abc-123")
record = await client.records.draft_records.read(record_url)

# Read Draft vs Published
draft = await client.records.draft_records.read("abc-123")
published = await client.records.published_records.read("abc-123")

# Read with Model Specification
datasets = client.records.with_model("datasets")
record = await datasets.draft_records.read("dataset-id")

# Read with Query Parameters
record = await client.records.draft_records.read("abc-123", query={"expand": "true"})
```

--------------------------------

### Select Default Repository with NRP Commandline Tool

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/commandline

Command to change the default repository used by the NRP commandline tool when no alias is specified.

```bash
nrp-cmd select repository <alias>
```

--------------------------------

### Jinja Template Include Example

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/templating

Illustrates how to use the Jinja `include` statement to embed the content of one template file into another, promoting code reuse for common components like headers.

```jinja
{# components/header.html #}
<header class="site-header">
  <h1>My Repository</h1>
</header>
```

```jinja
{# base/page.html #}
{% include "components/header.html" %}
<main>{% block content %}{% endblock %}</main>
```

--------------------------------

### Update Draft Records

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library_records

Demonstrates how to read a draft record, modify its metadata fields such as subjects and descriptions, and persist the changes back to the server.

```python
record = await client.records.draft_records.read("abc-123")

if "subjects" not in record.metadata:
    record.metadata["subjects"] = []
record.metadata["subjects"].append({"subject": "climate science"})

if "additional_descriptions" not in record.metadata:
    record.metadata["additional_descriptions"] = []
record.metadata["additional_descriptions"].append({
    "description": "This dataset contains...",
    "type": {"id": "methods"}
})

updated = await client.records.draft_records.update(record)
```

--------------------------------

### Example JSON for Nested Data Type

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/model_backend/model_reference

Provides a valid JSON example illustrating the structure for the 'nested' data type, showcasing 'contributors' and 'measurements' as examples of nested objects.

```json
{
  "contributors": {
    "name": "Prof. John Doe",
    "role": "supervisor",
    "affiliation": "Harvard University"
  },
  "measurements": {
    "parameter": "temperature",
    "value": 23.5,
    "unit": "celsius",
    "uncertainty": 0.1
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Custom Repository Access

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/userguide/python_library/async_library

Shows how to define custom repository configurations, including authentication tokens and TLS verification, instead of using the default file-based configuration.

```python
from nrp_cmd.config import Config, RepositoryConfig
from yarl import URL
 
# Create custom configuration
config = Config()
config.add_repository(RepositoryConfig(
    alias="my-repo",
    url=URL("https://your-repository.org/api"),
    token="your-access-token",
    verify_tls=True
))
 
# Use custom configuration
client = await get_async_client("my-repo", config=config)
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Webpack Alias for Translations

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/i18n

Configuration snippet for webpack.py to create an alias for the translation package, enabling easy imports in React components.

```python
from invenio_assets.webpack import WebpackThemeBundle
 
theme = WebpackThemeBundle(
    __name__,
    ".",
    default="semantic-ui",
    themes={
        "semantic-ui": dict(
            dependencies={},
            devDependencies={},
            aliases={
                "@translations/my_module": "translations/my_module"
            },
        )
    },
)
```

--------------------------------

### Implement UI Resource Component

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/model_ui/detail

Extends UIResourceComponent to inject custom context data into templates and registers the component within the UI resource configuration.

```python
from oarepo_ui.resources.components import UIResourceComponent
 
class MymodelDetailComponent(UIResourceComponent):
    def before_detail(self, resource, request, extra_context, **kwargs):
        # Add custom data to template context
        record = extra_context.get("record")
        if record:
            extra_context["related_records"] = self._get_related(record)
 
    def _get_related(self, record):
        # Fetch related records from service layer
        return []
```

```python
from .components import MymodelDetailComponent
 
class MymodelUIResourceConfig(RecordsUIResourceConfig):
    components = [
        MymodelDetailComponent,
    ]
```

--------------------------------

### Perform Full Webpack Asset Rebuild

Source: https://nrp-cz.github.io/docs/customize/repository_ui/webpack

Commands to execute for a complete rebuild of frontend assets, including cleaning previous builds and installing dependencies.

```bash
invenio webpack clean create
invenio webpack install
```
